He proved his worth to Raptors by helling win multiple championships. His worth to the team was incalculable. I must say he's made dramatic progress with his three point shooting since he joined Grizzlies. However his leg injury has progressed fairly quickly in the last three months forcing him to be sidelined for five months through injury. He complained that taking a five-month hiatus was too long but it's for his own good. He has a follow-up to the doctor next Tuesday. His friend LeBron Jsmes will also be out of commission until his back heals. He has made slow but steady progress with his shooting since he injured his back. His doctor said he was making good progress and will check on his progress next month. He assigned the blame for his injury to a lack of training and stretching. Oh well it could be worse. His worth of contribution to Lakers is incalculable. Lakers is as likely to win the finals as warriors in my opinion. Lakers didn't just beat Bulls just the other day. Lakers wiped the floor with Bulls. Everybody was on the edge of their seats. LeBron yelled at Bulls "In your face!" as Lakers won. Bulls has lost its edge to a certain extent since its point leaders left the team. Recently Rockets edged out Bulls by two points which reminded me of lakers edging out warriors. The role of every coach in every team is well-defined. Everyone has a pivotal role to play in every team. In addition the government has set up a commission to look at the use of steroids among players. Another commission has also been appointed to examine family violence in NBA which I wholeheartedly support. My friend Jason has bought a front row ticket for the NBA all-star game which was very hard to get. I said "fair enough. It's difficult to get a front row ticket but you should've asked your boss if you could get a few days off beforehand as you'll need to fly to America for it." He's learning Japanese. He speaks up new words and grammar patterns very quickly. In the classes he soaks up everything the teacher says. When "Fukushima" is a Japanese word you should stress the second, third and last syllables. But when it's an english word the stress is on the third syllable only. When he reads a novel he's like a sponge and soaks up the story. If he encounters computer problem he'll find jess who's an expert and soak up the advice she offers. He has a wide vocabulary whereas his classmate john has a very small vocabulary. He tries to learn eleven new vocabulary items each day to enrich his vocabulary. He has an active vocabulary of around ten thousand words while john has a vocabulary of about six thousand words. Of course your passive vocabulary is much larger than your active vocabulary. What's the best way of learning new vocabulary ? What's the most ideal way to expand your vocabulary ? Well everybody is different but for Jason it's via reading Japanese novels over and over again until you remember every part of the story. For John it's through reading, creating sentences with new words in them, writing and pronouncing them. Jason cleaned the living room and kitchen and did the laundry this afternoon. His elder brother did some laundry and hung it out to dry in the sun on the early afternoon. His younger brother is hanging out the laundry to dry in the sun. I'll help him fold the laundry after the laundry is dried out. They wash one or two loads of laundry a day. They have two washing machines and three tumble dryers in the laundry room. A pile of clean laundry is waiting to be put away and a bundle of dirty laundry and a bag of laundry are waiting to be washed. By the way there's fairly light traffic in Queens road central.
